title: Cock Up Your Beaver.
content:
Tune - "Cock up your beaver."


I.

        When first my brave Johnnie lad
            Came to this town,
        He had a blue bonnet
            That wanted the crown;
        But now he has gotten
            A hat and a feather,
        Hey, brave Johnnie lad,
            Cock up your beaver!

II.

        Cock up your beaver,
            And cock it fu' sprush,
        We'll over the border
            And gie them a brush;
        There's somebody there
            We'll teach better behaviour,
        Hey, brave Johnnie lad,
            Cock up your beaver!
meaning:
Robert Burns's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Cock Up Your Beaver."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
